featureName: Enabled With Zero Providers Reports False Ready
statusReason: Executable eight-scope per-directory planning and structured test/scenario handoffs are finalized. No local provider/runtime reproduction, implementation, behavior test, certification, production mutation, commit, push, or deployment is claimed.

scopeProgress.name: Shared API And Accessible UI Projections

executionHistory.summary: Reconciled the UX-owned recommendation-readiness contract across request, watches, and operator status surfaces, with shared availability/provenance/mutation primitives; distinct zero-provider, no-match, policy-filtered-empty, degraded, auth, quota, timeout, provider-error, retry, and unauthorized states; responsive/accessibility behavior; real-stack Playwright-visible outcomes; and routed design questions. No provider, mutation, browser or test execution, implementation, evidence, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment is claimed.

Reconciled design.md against current requirements, UX, recommendation provider/config/API/web/watch/store code, migrations, and tests. Defined explicit enabled/configured/provider-ready/degraded/unavailable semantics, production provider inventory, pre-persistence mutation gates, typed provider evidence, additive migration sequencing, API/UI/observability/rollback contracts, and bounded implementation seams. Artifact lint was executed separately; no implementation, product test, certification, status transition, source/test/spec/scope/report/uservalidation/scenario-manifest change, build, deploy, commit, or push is claimed.

Finalized eight dependency-ordered per-scope planning artifacts, 47 exact Test Plan rows with one-for-one DoD evidence items, scenario-manifest mappings, test-plan.json, report, and user-validation baseline. Required real provider-compatible Google/Yelp validation, production fixture rejection, typed unavailable/degraded/no-match/filtered/error states, watch pre-persistence refusal, migration/rollback, consumer tracing, no-interception Playwright, and stress. No requirements/design/source/test/certification/runtime/deploy/commit/push work is claimed.

Reconciled SR-008/GRILL-09: first readiness requires one operator-selected eligible healthy production provider; disabled/unconfigured/fixture/category-irrelevant providers are excluded from the denominator; a second provider is not a completion dependency; adapter choice remains provider-neutral because current production registry code is empty. Preserved SCN-039-005-01..09 and added 10. No source, config, provider, tests, design, scopes, report, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Reconciled the SR-008/GRILL-09 provider-readiness design and the requested SR-012/SR-013 cross-capability design obligations. One operator-selected eligible healthy production provider now satisfies first readiness; unused declarations are operator-only setup inventory outside the denominator; the empty production registry requires a provider-neutral SPI and seven-part first-adapter decision gate; a second provider is independent. Added validate-plane recommendations.readiness trace/SLO and disposable no-interception provider fault profiles. No spec, scopes, report, uservalidation, manifest, config, provider, source, test, certification, status transition, CCManager mutation, spec 079, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Spec-scope-hardening reconciliation after the 2026-07-24T05:46Z independent-review spec revision. Integrated the newly added spec Gherkin scenario SCN-039-005-10 (Disabled providers do not dilute readiness) into scenario-manifest.json, test-plan.json (REC04-TP07 denominator integration, REC07-TP08 projection e2e-ui), and scopes/04 + scopes/07 (Gherkin, Test Plan row, tiered DoD with 6->7 and 7->8 evidence parity). Renumbered the plan-decomposition scenario 'Provider foundation migrates' from SCN-039-005-10 to SCN-039-005-15 to free the canonical spec slot; updated scopes/01, scenario-manifest.json, and test-plan.json accordingly. Rebuilt certification.scopeProgress from one stale entry to the real eight-scope per-directory inventory and updated scopes/_index.md coverage. Marked the legacy single-file scopes.md superseded (do-not-execute) pointing at scopes/_index.md. design.md is current (2026-07-24T05:46Z) and already grounds the disabled-provider denominator/first-readiness behavior, so no design residual arises for this packet. Status held in_progress; no source, config, provider, test, certification, runtime, CCManager, spec 079, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

F2 (independent-review LOW-MED) design-side remediation: updated design.md to cite BUG-070-001's MutationTrustGuard (AUTH-011) as the single owning same-origin + session-bound anti-CSRF guard that this packet's cookie-authenticated recommendation mutations BIND to (API Contracts and Security And Privacy sections). Removed the prior additive/parallel-guard framing; 039 consumes the 070-owned guard and does not implement its own. ROUTED to bubbles.plan: add specDependsOn += specs/070-web-username-password-login/bugs/BUG-070-001-production-credential-session-paseto-split (dependency edge is plan-owned). Guards artifact-lint.sh and traceability-guard.sh re-run before/after with identical verdicts; no regression. Read-only reference to BUG-070-001 design; BUG-070-001 not edited. No spec, scopes, scenario-manifest, test-plan, report, uservalidation, source, test, certification, status transition, commit, push, or deployment change is claimed.

statusDiscipline.scopePickupRule: All dependsOn scopes must be Done. Pick the lowest-numbered eligible scope.
